findings.headline: Of 35,364 CVEs published in 2026, only 86 (0.24%) are on CISA's KEV list so far
findings.detail: A floor that rises as the cohort ages, not a final rate. Confirmed exploitation stays rare while volume climbs, the whole triage story.

findings.headline: 2026 year-to-date (35,364) already exceeds every full year before 2024
findings.detail: Six-plus months of 2026 outrank all of 2023 (28,817) and every year prior.

findings.headline: June 2026 was the busiest CVE month ever: 7,454
findings.detail: No calendar month in the program's history published more CVEs.

findings.headline: Only 47 of 2026's 35,364 CVEs (0.13%) score EPSS >= 0.5
findings.detail: 222 (0.6%) clear EPSS 0.1. Predicted exploitation is as rare as confirmed: model v2026.06.15.

findings.headline: Patchstack published 609 more CVEs this month than last
findings.detail: A sharp month-over-month surge worth a closer look.

findings.headline: CVE-2026-43284 has the highest EPSS of 2026 not on KEV: 0.932
findings.detail: linux / linux_kernel. High predicted risk the confirmed list hasn't caught up to.

findings.headline: CISA added 24 CVEs to KEV in the last 30 days, 2 ransomware-linked
findings.detail: Exploitation news travels; a fresh KEV batch is always timely.

findings.headline: GitHub_M is 2026's top CVE issuer with 6,801 (19% of all published)
findings.detail: The CNA mix reflects who has scope, not who ships the worst code.

findings.headline: The busiest CVE weekday in 2026 is Wednesday (not Tuesday)
findings.detail: Wednesday: 7,943 vs Tuesday: 7,216. The 'Patch Tuesday' mental model does not match the data.

findings.headline: 35,364 CVEs published so far in 2026, one every 7.4 minutes
findings.detail: 195/day, +49% vs 2025 at the same point. Straight-line run-rate lands near 71,300 for the full year.

findings.headline: The CVE catalog is at 344,258, 5,742 short of 350,000
findings.detail: At the current rate it crosses 350,000 in about 29 days.

findings.headline: The single biggest CVE day of 2026: 2026-06-09 with 747
findings.detail: Batch publishing by high-volume CNAs drives these spikes.
